ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 covers hamburger as a general class. No specific Jollibee Jolliburger entry exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJW covers hamburger as a generic category. No brand-specific Jolliburger code exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for a branded fast-food beef burger of this specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One Classic Jolliburger single patty, approximately 130 g, consisting of a beef patty in a soft bun with lettuce, tomato, mayonnaise, and ketchup.

nutrient_source.title: Hamburger, single patty, plain — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21101)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for a plain fast-food hamburger with single beef patty (NDB 21101 / FDC ID 171938). The Jolliburger is a comparable single-patty beef burger on a soft bun with condiments. Values are representative for a standard fast-food single-patty beef burger per 100 g edible portion. Sodium is adjusted upward slightly to reflect typical condiment contribution consistent with USDA Branded Food data for comparable products.

gi_source.title: Hamburger bun and beef burger composite GI — University of Sydney GI Database; white bread bun GI reference
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Jolliburger. GI of 61 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for a standard hamburger on a white-flour bun (GI ~61, medium category), which is the closest analogous food. The bun contributes the dominant glycemic load; the beef patty and condiments contribute negligible GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (61 × 35.1) / 100 ≈ 21.4, using serving size 130 g and carbohydrate_g per 100 g of 27.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~624 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of the Classic Jolliburger Single Patty due to its high sodium content (~624 mg per serving), which represents approximately 27% of the WHO recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg; frequent intake may contribute to elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 61) and moderate glycemic load (~21.4 per serving) from refined white-flour bun
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume the Classic Jolliburger Single Patty with caution, as its medium GI (61) and glycemic load of approximately 21.4 per serving can produce a moderate postprandial blood glucose rise; pairing with non-starchy vegetables and limiting portion frequency is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.8 g per 100 g) and trans fat (~0.3 g per 100 g) content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake of the Classic Jolliburger Single Patty due to its saturated fat (~4.9 g per serving) and trans fat (~0.4 g per serving) content, which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels when consumed regularly as part of a high-fat dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Classic Jolliburger Single Patty is a fast-food beef burger sold by Jollibee, a Filipino multinational fast-food chain with outlets in Singapore including Jollibee Bugis Village. The burger consists of a seasoned, grilled single beef patty served in a soft white-flour bun, garnished with fresh lettuce, tomato slices, mayonnaise, and ketchup. The flavour profile is savoury and mildly sweet, with a tender beef patty and a soft, slightly sweet bun characteristic of Jollibee's Filipino-influenced fast-food style. The texture combines a juicy, well-seasoned patty with a pillowy bun and crisp lettuce. Nutritionally, the burger provides approximately 254 kcal per 100 g, with 13.5 g protein, 10.2 g fat (including 3.8 g saturated fat), and 27.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g. A standard single serving of approximately 130 g delivers around 330 kcal. The sodium content is notable at approximately 480 mg per 100 g (~624 mg per serving), making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The medium glycemic index (GI ~61) and glycemic load (~21.4 per serving) are relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The burger is certified Halal at Jollibee Singapore outlets. It is classified as ultra-processed food and is best consumed occasionally as part of a balanced diet. The Jolliburger is a signature menu item of Jollibee and is widely recognised across Southeast Asia and among Filipino diaspora communities.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Jolliburger?
What is the GI of a Jollibee burger?

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional data from Jollibee Singapore or an approved primary regional source (HPB Singapore Foodo) was available for the Classic Jolliburger Single Patty. Values are adapted from the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for a comparable plain fast-food single-patty hamburger (FDC ID 171938), which is the closest approved source match. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous hamburger entry. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. Trans fat is present at approximately 0.3 g per 100 g from the beef patty; this is a naturally occurring trans fat (vaccenic acid / conjugated linoleic acid) rather than industrially produced trans fat, consistent with USDA data for beef-containing fast-food burgers. The '(D)' designation in the original field survey listing likely refers to a delivery-specific menu variant or bundle designation and does not affect the nutritional profile of the burger itself. Serving size of 130 g is estimated based on typical single-patty fast-food burger weights consistent with USDA reference data. Glycemic load per serving = (61 × 27.0 × 1.30) / 100 ≈ 21.4. All -1 sentinel values are documented above per g3 requirements.

Added health_context for all three required conditions with specific clinical guidance.
